architecture_fit.note: Solves knapsack to 0.997 optimality, but is a 64-step iterative swap local-search dressed as top-1 attention — far from a base_model.py delta (no embedding/residual/MLP, no learning).

baseline_comparison.note: Greedy ratio strawman measured on identical batches; circuit provably >= greedy and beats it at all five sweep fracs, though greedy is already strong so the lift is marginal.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built circuit with no learned model to ablate; README proposes the right ablations (zero feasibility mask, drop delta>0 gate) but does not actually run them.

operating_range.note: Holds across the full goal-specified capacity_frac sweep 0.3-0.7 with feasible_rate=1.0 everywhere, but that span is <1 order of magnitude.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set circuit, no training — the mechanism is written out explicitly and reproduces near-optimal behaviour.

visual_judgement.note: Two clean panels: optimality-vs-capacity with greedy + exact-optimum reference lines, and per-frac lift bars annotated with feasibility — claim is legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties axes to the goal's two sub-questions (how close to optimal, how robust across the budget sweep) with the right baseline and grain.

notes: A well-engineered hand-built solver that reaches near-optimal, fully-feasible knapsack solutions and cleanly beats the greedy baseline across the whole capacity sweep, fixing the prior pass's sub-greedy/infeasible failures. Its main weaknesses are architectural fidelity (a 64-step iterative local search only loosely framed as attention, with no learning and little resemblance to base_model.py) and the absence of actual ablations to back the causal claims. Strong on the hardcoded-circuit bonus and visualisation, but the mean of scored items lands below 4.
